description_long: This is a hand tool featuring a robust wooden handle and a dual-ended metal head. One end of the head forms a broad, curved axe blade with a sharp edge, while the opposite end tapers into a pointed pick. The handle is slightly curved, likely for ergonomic grip and improved leverage during use. The metal head appears securely mounted onto the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged fit. The tool's surfaces show a matte finish, with the wood displaying a natural grain and the metal a dark, slightly roughened texture. This type of tool is commonly used for chopping, splitting, or prying in urban maintenance, gardening, or emergency scenarios.
description: A dual-ended axe with a curved wooden handle and a metal head featuring a broad blade and a pointed pick. The tool has a matte, natural finish and is designed for chopping or prying tasks.
description_view_0: The broad, curved blade and pointed pick are both visible, with the handle's curvature and wood grain clearly shown.
description_view_1: A direct end-on view showing the narrow profile of the blade and pick, with the handle's round cross-section visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the axe, again showing the blade and pick, with the handle's curve and attachment to the head in focus.
description_view_3: A top-down view highlighting the alignment of the blade and pick, and the circular end of the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a round cross-section, natural grain, and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, curved blade with a sharp edge on one side and a pointed pick on the opposite side; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit with the metal head mounted onto the wooden handle.
